summary: The Goshen Township Board of Trustees held a regular meeting covering ARP fund project updates, zoning resolutions, and infrastructure planning. Major discussions included design services for Fire Station 18 sewer/water/parking lot repairs, road paving priorities, service garage renovation planning, and multiple nuisance/junk vehicle abatement resolutions. The board also discussed community signage, electronic signs, parking lot lighting, and the Brownfield remediation grant application the township will pursue independently after county commissioners declined to complete their part.
decisions: Approved payment of bills for February 23 through March 8, 2022
Approved contract with Choice One Engineering not to exceed $30,000 for Fire Station 18 sewer, water, and parking lot repair design using ARP funds
Accepted resignation of part-time firefighter/EMT Jacob Spears effective February 2022
Accepted State Fire Marshal's training reimbursement grant of $565
Accepted Ohio Department of Public Safety pediatric EMS grant up to $1,500
Approved Resolution R-013-2022 denying zone change request for 1867 Kerber Road by Fisher Holmes
Approved Resolution R-014-2022 authorizing application to Ohio Department of Development for Brownfield Remediation Program grant
Approved Resolution R-015-2022 for abatement/removal of nuisance at 1779 Parker Road
Approved Resolution R-016-2022 approving preliminary development plan for 6600 Patricia Boulevard
Approved Resolution R-017-2022 determining junk motor vehicle at 1503 State Route 28
Approved Resolution R-018-2022 determining junk motor vehicle at 1503 State Route 28
Approved Resolution R-019-2022 determining junk motor vehicle at 1503 State Route 28
Tabled Item O (Choice One contract review) until next trustee meeting
Approved indigent burial of Tamara Hartley at a cost of $703
Awarded mowing bid for Goshen and Myers cemeteries to Walker Landscape and Lawn Care at $1,225 per cut for one year
action_items: Administrator Peoples and Chad to continue getting cost estimates for road paving options and bring back to board
Administrator Peoples to bring roof and facade project details and quotes for admin building to next meeting
Director Holbrook to independently pursue Brownfield Remediation Program grant application for Goshen Township
Director Holbrook to have prosecutor review Choice One contract and bring back at next meeting
Chad to get pricing on new township boundary/welcome signs and all hardware
Staff to investigate electronic sign options for Fire Station 18 and Station 19 on Route 48
Staff to get updated pricing on parking lot lighting improvements including LED conversion
Chief Myers to share link for Captain Allen and Lieutenant Estep's State Fire Marshal valor award ceremony
Administrator Peoples to coordinate walk-over of fire station property for electronic sign placement before parking lot paving
Director Holbrook to work with engineering group on Brownfield grant application for Combs properties
Future discussion needed on service garage renovation vs. new construction and long-term financing options
key_amounts: $30,000 - not to exceed contract with Choice One Engineering for Fire Station 18 design services
$565 - State Fire Marshal's training reimbursement grant
Up to $1,500 - Ohio Department of Public Safety pediatric EMS grant
$703 - indigent burial cost for Tamara Hartley
$1,225 - per cut cost for cemetery mowing by Walker Landscape
$500,000 - approximately 50% road paving match from ODOT
$2,100 - estimated cost for 30 township boundary signs
$60,000 - previous quote for monument sign with electronic sign board
$250,000 - service department 2022 budget for road paving
$15,000 - previous estimate for parking lot lighting upgrade
$2.5 million - Milford's public works garage cost four years ago (comparison)
$200,000 - school electronic sign cost mentioned as comparison
notable_topics: Fire Station 18 sewer, water line replacement and parking lot repaving project
ARP (American Rescue Plan) fund project planning and prioritization
Road paving options and priorities for 2022
Service department garage renovation vs. new construction long-term planning
Township boundary/welcome signage with new logo
Electronic message signs for Fire Station 18 and Station 19
Brownfield Remediation Program grant - township pursuing independently after county commissioner issues
Nuisance abatement and junk vehicle resolutions for Combs properties
Preliminary development plan approval for 6600 Patricia Boulevard
Zone change denial for 1867 Kerber Road
Park district property transfer to Clermont County
Local government fund dispute with City of Lublin and City of Milford
Parking lot lighting and security concerns
Captain Allen and Lieutenant Estep receiving State Fire Marshal valor award
Disappointment with County Engineer's road budget excluding Goshen Township
notes: Local government fund resolution from previous meeting will be rescinded due to failure to invite City of Lublin as required by prior agreement with commissioners; new resolution expected in April after proper meetings. Clermont County commissioners declined to complete their portion of the Brownfield grant application, with one commissioner recusing herself due to connection to the property; township proceeding independently. The building mortgage will be paid off in 2028, which factors into long-term service garage planning. Multiple junk vehicle resolutions were for the same Combs industrial property at 1503 State Route 28; prosecutor agreed future resolutions can list multiple vehicles on one resolution. Jacob Spears left for a full-time career position at another fire department, noted as an ongoing staffing trend.
